attachment.planContent:
# Plan: Add SGLang and TensorRT-LLM Serving Backends

## Context

The project currently benchmarks workflow-orchestrating LLM agents through a **vLLM-only** local-serving path, with one auxiliary BiFrost gateway path for frontier-API comparisons. To get an unbiased view of throughput / latency / concurrency for the same models, we want to benchmark them against **SGLang** and **TensorRT-LLM** in addition to vLLM.

**Why this is feasible without a deep refactor.** Phase-1 exploration confirmed the codebase already has the right seam: every model YAML carries a `serving.engine` field (37 files: 36 `vllm`, 1 `bifrost`), `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` already dispatches on `SERVING_ENGINE` for the BiFrost branch, both eval CLIs (`agent_benchmark.py`, `concurrency_benchmark.py`) accept `--engine` as metadata, and **all callers reach the model over OpenAI-compatible HTTP/SSE** — so any backend exposing `/v1/chat/completions` on port 8000 is a drop-in.

**vLLM-coupled code that does NOT extend** (keep as-is): `src/llm_workflow_agents/vllm_plugin.py`, `serving/launch_vllm_turboquant.py`, `serving/launch_vllm_rotorquant.py`, `quantization/{turboquant,rotorquant}/vllm_integration.py`. The TurboQuant / RotorQuant kernels are vLLM-internal and don't translate. **Phase-3 KV-quant matrix (`run_exp_d.sh`) stays vLLM-only by design.**

**User-confirmed scope decisions** (March 2026):
- Phase 3 KV-quant matrix → **vLLM-only**.
- TRT-LLM serving → **JIT-from-HF first**; pre-built engines via `trtllm-build` deferred to a follow-up.
- Initial coverage → **all 14 Cat A + 5 Cat B/C models** get SGLang + TRT-LLM YAMLs (~38 new files).
- Install path → **venv-only**, matching the existing `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer` pattern.

**Outcome.** `run_exp_a.sh` and `run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` can drive vLLM, SGLang, and TRT-LLM by setting `serving.engine` in the YAML. Existing 36 vLLM YAMLs and current scripts behave byte-for-byte identically.

---

## Architecture seam

```
                ┌─ serving/launch.sh ─┐    (NEW: engine dispatcher; reads YAML)
                │                     │
   YAML ──────► │  case engine in     │ ──► serving/launch_vllm.sh   (existing, unchanged)
                │     vllm  ──────────┼───► serving/launch_sglang.sh (NEW)
                │     sglang ─────────┼───► serving/launch_trtllm.sh (NEW)
                │     tensorrt_llm ──►│
                └─────────────────────┘
                                          ↓ all expose :8000 OpenAI-compat
   eval/agent_benchmark.py ──► /v1/chat/completions ──► (any backend)
   eval/concurrency_benchmark.py ──► /v1/chat/completions ──► (any backend)
```

Three environments, mutually-exclusive (torch / vLLM / transformers pin conflicts):

| Venv          | Bootstrap                        | Serves         |
|---------------|----------------------------------|----------------|
| `.venv-infer` | `scripts/install_infer.sh`       | vLLM 0.20      |
| `.venv-sglang`| `scripts/install_sglang.sh` (NEW)| SGLang 0.5.11  |
| `.venv-trtllm`| `scripts/install_trtllm.sh` (NEW)| TensorRT-LLM 1.2.1 |

---

## File-by-file change list

### New files

**Install scripts** (mirror `scripts/install_infer.sh`, idempotent, smoke-test at end):
- `scripts/install_sglang.sh` — `.venv-sglang`, Python 3.12, `uv pip install sglang>=0.5.11`, torch≥2.11+cu130 from existing `pytorch-cu130` index. Smoke: `python -c "import sglang; print(sglang.__version__)"`.
- `scripts/install_trtllm.sh` — `.venv-trtllm`, Python 3.12, `uv pip install tensorrt_llm==1.2.1`, torch==2.10.0+cu130 pinned via constraint file. Pre-flight `dpkg -l libopenmpi-dev` check; aborts with hint if missing. Pre-flight `nvidia-smi` CUDA 13 check; warn if older. Smoke: `python -c "import tensorrt_llm; print(tensorrt_llm.__version__)"`.

**Launcher scripts** (mirror `serving/launch_vllm.sh`, force `--port 8000`):
- `serving/launch.sh` — dispatcher; reads `serving.engine` from YAML, `exec`s `serving/launch_${engine}.sh "$@"`. Single dispatch table for backend N+1.
- `serving/launch_sglang.sh` — sources `.venv-sglang`, `exec python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path … --port 8000`. KV-cache vocab guard: rejects `turboquant_*`/`rotorquant*`/`kivi_*`/`kvquant*` with a clear error. Health endpoint `/health`.
- `serving/launch_trtllm.sh` — sources `.venv-trtllm`, `exec trtllm-serve <hf_id> --port 8000 --max_batch_size … --max_num_tokens …`. JIT-from-HF only in this PR; warns on runtime `--kv-cache-dtype=fp8` (TRT-LLM is build-time only). Health endpoint `/health`.
- `serving/_yaml_helper.sh` — extracted `parse_yaml()` from `launch_vllm.sh:48-65`, sourced by all three launchers (de-duplication).

**Per-(model, backend) YAML configs** in existing dirs (suffix matches the existing `_fp8` precedent at `qwen36_27b_fp8.yaml`):
- `configs/models_exp_a/<model>_sglang.yaml` × 14 (gemma3_27b, qwen3_32b, qwen35_35b_a3b, qwen36_35b_a3b, qwen36_35b_a3b_fp8, qwen36_27b, qwen36_27b_fp8, mistral_24b, nemotron_30b†, glm47_flash, gemma4_26b_a4b, gemma4_31b, gemma4_e4b, gemma4_e2b)
- `configs/models_exp_a/<model>_trtllm.yaml` × 14 (same set)
- `configs/models_exp_bc/<model>_sglang.yaml` × 5 (qwen25_3b, qwen35_4b, glm47_flash_small, gemma_2b, gemma3_4b)
- `configs/models_exp_bc/<model>_trtllm.yaml` × 5 (same set)

  † Nemotron-3-Nano (Mamba hybrid) likely won't load on either backend; ship the YAML but mark `serving.skip_reason: "mamba hybrid not supported by sglang/trtllm in 1.2.x"` and let the runner skip it. Same R6 pattern as the existing vLLM Nemotron handling.

**Tests:**
- `tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py` — verifies `serving/launch.sh` correctly dispatches by `serving.engine` and each launcher script exists + is executable. Pure shell parsing, no GPU.

**Lock files** (optional but recommended):
- `requirements-sglang.txt`, `requirements-trtllm.txt` — `uv pip freeze` snapshot for CI reproducibility.

### Edited files

| Path | Edit | Reference lines |
|------|------|-----------------|
| `pyproject.toml` | Append `[sglang]` and `[trtllm]` optional-dep groups (after `[infer]` block at L74-85). Documentation-as-code only — install scripts remain the supported path because TRT-LLM needs apt-level `libopenmpi-dev`. | append after L85 |
| `scripts/run_exp_a.sh` | (1) `LAUNCH_SCRIPT="$PROJECT_ROOT/serving/launch.sh"` (was `launch_vllm.sh`). (2) Resolve `SERVING_ENGINE` from YAML inside the loop. (3) Rename `VLLM_PID` → `SERVER_PID` (cosmetic). (4) Result-file naming includes engine: `${MODEL_NAME//\//_}_${SERVING_ENGINE}_${KV_CACHE_DTYPE}.json`. (5) Pass `--engine $SERVING_ENGINE` to eval. (6) Add optional `--backend {vllm\|sglang\|tensorrt_llm}` flag that swaps the MODEL_CONFIGS array to the `_sglang.yaml` / `_trtllm.yaml` siblings. Backward-compatible: omitting `--backend` runs the existing vLLM path. | L21, L43-54, L65-70, L88-105, L108-116 |
| `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` | (1) `LAUNCH_SCRIPT=…/launch.sh`. (2) Generalize the per-engine defaults block (currently `if bifrost…else vllm`) to a `case` covering `vllm\|sglang\|tensorrt_llm` (same defaults as vLLM) plus existing `bifrost`. (3) Result-file naming gains the engine token. (4) For TRT-LLM, bump the health-poll budget when `SERVING_ENGINE == "tensorrt_llm"` from 90×5s = 450s to 360×5s = 30min (JIT first-launch can take 5–15 min). (5) Bump warmup requests to 32 for TRT-LLM to absorb prefill warmup. | L51, L190-198, L204-208, L231-280 |
| `scripts/run_exp_d.sh` | **No code change.** Add a 2-line comment near L16 stating Phase-3 KV-quant matrix is intentionally vLLM-only (custom quant kernels live in `src/llm_workflow_agents/vllm_plugin.py`). | L16 (comment only) |
| `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/agent_benchmark.py` | Widen `--engine choices=[…]` from `["vllm","bifrost"]` to `["vllm","bifrost","sglang","tensorrt_llm"]`. Inside `_call_vllm` (L273-321): change the `if engine == "vllm"` guard around `chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking` to `if engine in {"vllm","sglang"}` (SGLang ≥0.5 accepts the field for Qwen3 thinking). Keep the bifrost-specific `_downgrade_tool_turns_to_text` guard at L306-307 unchanged. Update help text + docstring. | L671, L673-677, L283, L316 |
| `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/concurrency_benchmark.py` | Widen `--engine choices`. Replace `sample_vram = engine == "vllm"` (L366) with `sample_vram = engine in {"vllm","sglang","tensorrt_llm"}` so VRAM is sampled for any local backend. | L520, L522, L344-364, L366 |
| `README.md` | New "Serving Backends" section between "Repository Structure" and "Setup". Backend comparison table; install-then-launch quick start; per-backend caveats (TRT-LLM apt deps, KV-quant vocabulary differences, mutually-exclusive venvs). | new section after L99 |

---

## YAML schema per backend

Existing vLLM YAML (unchanged):
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "hermes"
  chat_template: "qwen2.5"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
```

New SGLang YAML:
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "sglang"
  tool_call_parser: "qwen25"          # SGLang vocab differs from vLLM
  chat_template: "qwen2.5"
  mem_fraction_static: 0.90
  context_length: 8192
  max_running_requests: 256
  attention_backend: "flashinfer"     # or "triton" if flashinfer build flakes
  port: 8000
  kv_cache_dtype: "auto"              # auto | fp8_e5m2 | fp8_e4m3
```

New TRT-LLM YAML (JIT-from-HF mode):
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "tensorrt_llm"
  tool_call_parser: null              # client-side post-parse <tool_call>{…}</tool_call>
  source: "hf"                         # JIT-from-HF; "engine_dir" mode is a follow-up PR
  port: 8000
  max_batch_size: 256
  max_num_tokens: 8192
  tp_size: 1
  kv_cache_dtype: "auto"               # auto only at runtime; fp8 requires trtllm-build
```

---

## Backend installation specifics

**SGLang 0.5.11** (`docs.sglang.io/get_started/install.html`, PyPI):
- `uv pip install sglang>=0.5.11` — FlashInfer + flashattention auto-installed.
- Python ≥3.10 (use 3.12 for consistency).
- Compatible with torch ≥2.11+cu130.
- Default port 30000 → forced to 8000 in launcher.
- Tool-call parser names: `qwen25`, `mistral`, `llama3`, `pythonic`. Document mapping in README. SGLang lacks dedicated parsers for `qwen3_coder` and `gemma4` — fall back to `pythonic` or post-parse client-side.

**TensorRT-LLM 1.2.1** (`nvidia.github.io/TensorRT-LLM/installation/linux.html`):
- `uv pip install tensorrt_llm==1.2.1` (no `--extra-index-url` needed for 1.2.x; PyPI direct).
- **Apt prerequisite**: `sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev` (cannot be satisfied by pip).
- CUDA toolkit 13.1 (host driver 575+; matches existing `install_train.sh` / `Dockerfile` defaults).
- torch==2.10.0+cu130 pinned via constraint file (`pip3 install torch==2.10.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu130`).
- Python 3.10 or 3.12.
- `trtllm-serve` exposes OpenAI-compat at port 8000 by default.
- KV-cache fp8 is **build-time only** via `trtllm-build --kv_cache_type fp8`; runtime overrides are ignored.

---

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| FlashInfer wheel lags cu130 | Default `attention_backend: triton` in YAMLs; flashinfer is opt-in. |
| SGLang tool-call parser vocab differs from vLLM | Per-backend YAMLs avoid translation; README documents the mapping. |
| TRT-LLM JIT first launch (5–15 min) trips eval health-poll timeout | Health-poll budget raised to 30 min when `SERVING_ENGINE == "tensorrt_llm"`. |
| TRT-LLM `libopenmpi-dev` apt requirement | `install_trtllm.sh` checks `dpkg -l` and aborts with apt-install hint. |
| Multi-process backends (SGLang router+workers, TRT-LLM mpirun) orphan children on kill | `cleanup()` uses `kill -- -$SERVER_PID` (process-group kill) plus a `pkill -f` fallback. |
| Nemotron-3-Nano (Mamba) won't load on SGLang/TRT-LLM | YAMLs ship with `serving.skip_reason`; runner shells skip with a logged warning. |
| Chat-template silent drift across backends biases benchmarks | Pin `--chat-template` explicitly in every launcher; README calls this out as a comparison invariant. |
| VRAM accounting (nvidia-smi `memory.used`) is device-total, not per-process | Documented as a known limitation; no code change. |

---

## Sequencing (suggested PR breakdown)

1. **PR 1 — Plumbing only** (no new backend yet). New `serving/launch.sh` dispatcher + `_yaml_helper.sh`; refactor `run_exp_a.sh` and `run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` to use it; widen `--engine` choices in both eval CLIs. Verify vLLM workflow byte-for-byte unchanged.
2. **PR 2 — SGLang.** `install_sglang.sh`, `launch_sglang.sh`, all 19 SGLang YAMLs, README "Serving Backends" section. Smoke test on Qwen2.5-3B.
3. **PR 3 — TensorRT-LLM (JIT mode).** `install_trtllm.sh`, `launch_trtllm.sh`, all 19 TRT-LLM YAMLs, README updates for TRT-LLM. Smoke test on Qwen2.5-3B.
4. **PR 4 — TRT-LLM pre-built engines (follow-up).** `scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh`, `source: engine_dir` mode in launcher, fp8 KV-cache support.

This ordering keeps each PR < 600 LOC and independently shippable / revertable.

---

## Critical files to modify

- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/serving/launch_vllm.sh` (reference; not edited)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_exp_a.sh` (edit)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh` (edit)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_exp_d.sh` (comment only)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/agent_benchmark.py` (widen `--engine`)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/concurrency_benchmark.py` (widen `--engine`, VRAM gate)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/pyproject.toml` (add `[sglang]`, `[trtllm]` extras)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/README.md` (new Serving Backends section)

## Existing utilities to reuse

- `parse_yaml()` from `serving/launch_vllm.sh:48-65` → extract to `serving/_yaml_helper.sh`, source from all three launchers.
- Engine-conditional dispatch shape in `scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh:137-149,190-198` → extend the existing `case`/`if` rather than rewriting.
- `_PeakVramSampler` in `eval/concurrency_benchmark.py:132-172` → already engine-aware via the `sample_vram` predicate; just widen it.
- Eval HTTP client `_call_vllm` / `_stream_request` already speak OpenAI/SSE — **no changes needed downstream of the launcher.**
- BiFrost engine guard pattern (`if engine == "bifrost":`) at `eval/agent_benchmark.py:306,316` → same shape used to guard the new `if engine in {"vllm","sglang"}` thinking-toggle branch.

---

## Verification plan

### A. Smoke test — fastest end-to-end signal per backend
Smallest model: `Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct` (already in `configs/models_exp_bc/qwen25_3b.yaml`).

```bash
# SGLang
./scripts/install_sglang.sh
./serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_bc/qwen25_3b_sglang.yaml &
SERVER_PID=$!
until curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health; do sleep 2; done
.venv-sglang/bin/python -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark \
    --model "Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct" --engine sglang --max-samples 5 \
    --output /tmp/sglang_smoke.json
kill -- -$SERVER_PID
jq '.engine == "sglang" and (.samples | length) == 5' /tmp/sglang_smoke.json  # expect true

# TRT-LLM (same pattern; expect ~10 min first launch)
./scripts/install_trtllm.sh
./serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_bc/qwen25_3b_trtllm.yaml &
# … same as above, --engine tensorrt_llm
```

### B. Phase 1 parity (proves runner shells dispatch correctly)
```bash
./scripts/run_exp_a.sh --backend sglang --max-samples 50 --dry-run    # confirm dispatch
./scripts/run_exp_a.sh --backend sglang --max-samples 50               # one model, 50 samples
# expect: results/exp_a/Qwen_Qwen3-32B_sglang_auto.json with "engine": "sglang"
```

### C. Phase 3 parity
```bash
./scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh \
    configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml \
    --concurrency-levels 1,4,16 --requests-per-level 8
# expect: results/concurrency/Qwen_Qwen3-32B_sglang_auto.json with engine == "sglang"
```

### D. vLLM regression test (must not change)
```bash
./scripts/run_exp_a.sh --max-samples 10
diff <(jq -S 'keys' results/exp_a/<old_run>.json) \
     <(jq -S 'keys' results/exp_a/<new_run>.json)   # schema unchanged
```

### E. Unit tests
```bash
pytest tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py -v
# verifies serving/launch.sh dispatches each engine correctly
```

## Setup

**Requirements:** Python 3.11+, CUDA 13.0, NVIDIA H100 (80 GB recommended)

### Two-venv layout

Training and inference pin mutually-incompatible versions of `torch`,
`transformers`, and `vllm`, so the project ships with **two separate
virtualenvs**:

| Venv | Purpose | Key pins |
|------|---------|----------|
| `.venv-train` | Phase 2 SFT + GRPO (Unsloth) | torch 2.10.0+cu130, vllm 0.19.1+cu130, transformers 4.57.6, trl 0.24.0, unsloth 2026.4.x |
| `.venv-infer` | Phase 1/3/4 serving + benchmarks (vLLM) | torch 2.11.0+cu130, vllm 0.20.0, transformers 5.6.2 |

Each venv is bootstrapped by its own installer script:

```bash
# Install uv (if not already installed)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Bootstrap the training environment (.venv-train)

---

## Setup
input.new_string:
---

## Serving Backends

The project supports three local serving backends. Every backend exposes an OpenAI-compatible API on port 8000, so all eval scripts work unchanged across them.

| Backend | Venv | Installer | Launcher |
|---------|------|-----------|----------|
| vLLM 0.20 | `.venv-infer` | `scripts/install_infer.sh` | `serving/launch_vllm.sh` |
| SGLang 0.5.11 | `.venv-sglang` | `scripts/install_sglang.sh` | `serving/launch_sglang.sh` |
| TensorRT-LLM 1.2.1 | `.venv-trtllm` | `scripts/install_trtllm.sh` | `serving/launch_trtllm.sh` |

The venvs are **mutually exclusive** (torch pin conflicts). Never activate two at once.

`serving/launch.sh` is the unified dispatcher — it reads `serving.engine` from the model YAML and execs the right backend launcher automatically.

### Quick start

```bash
# Install a backend (one-time)
./scripts/install_sglang.sh      # or install_trtllm.sh

# Start the server using the dispatcher
./serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_bc/qwen25_3b_sglang.yaml

# Run the benchmark (engine is read from the YAML)
source .venv-sglang/bin/activate
python -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark \
    --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct --engine sglang \
    --output results/smoke_sglang.json

# Or use the experiment runner with --backend
./scripts/run_exp_a.sh --backend sglang --max-samples 50
```

For TensorRT-LLM, the first launch performs JIT compilation from HF weights (~5–15 min). Subsequent launches reuse the cached engine. The health-poll budget is automatically extended to 30 min.

### Per-backend model YAMLs

Each model has per-backend YAML variants following the naming convention `<model>_<backend>.yaml`:

```
configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_sglang.yaml
configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml
configs/models_exp_bc/qwen25_3b_sglang.yaml
configs/models_exp_bc/qwen25_3b_trtllm.yaml
```

### Caveats

**TensorRT-LLM apt prerequisite:** `sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev` must be installed before running `install_trtllm.sh`. The script checks for it and exits with a hint if it's missing.

**KV-cache quantization vocabulary:** The vLLM-only quant strings (`turboquant_*`, `rotorquant_*`, `kivi_*`, `kvquant*`) are rejected by `launch_sglang.sh` and `launch_trtllm.sh` with a clear error. Phase 3 KV-quant benchmarks (`run_exp_d.sh`) are intentionally vLLM-only.

**SGLang tool-call parsers:** SGLang uses different parser names than vLLM. The per-backend YAMLs already carry the correct value — do not copy `tool_call_parser` between vLLM and SGLang configs.

| vLLM parser | SGLang parser |
|-------------|---------------|
| `hermes`, `qwen3_coder` | `qwen25` |
| `mistral` | `mistral` |
| `gemma`, `gemma4`, `glm4`, `nemotron` | `pythonic` |

**Nemotron-3-Nano (Mamba hybrid):** Not supported by SGLang or TRT-LLM in their current versions. The YAMLs carry `serving.skip_reason` and the runner shells skip them automatically.

---

## Setup

content.text: Let me also check if there's an existing scripts section that lists installers, so we can mention the new ones:

content.content:
183	
184	```bash
185	# Install uv (if not already installed)
186	curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
187	
188	# Bootstrap the training environment (.venv-train)
189	./scripts/install_train.sh
190	
191	# Bootstrap the inference / serving environment (.venv-infer)
192	./scripts/install_infer.sh
193	```
194	
195	The installers call `uv venv` + `uv pip install -e ".[train,dev]"` (or
196	`".[infer,dev]"`) using the corresponding extras defined in
197	`pyproject.toml`. `scripts/install_train.sh` additionally pulls the
198	`vllm-0.19.1+cu130` wheel from the GitHub release (PyPI only ships the
199	cu129 build).
200	
201	#### Unsloth pre-built container (`Dockerfile.unsloth`)
202	
203	When using `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth` (based on `unsloth/unsloth`),
204	the container already ships torch, Unsloth, vLLM, and trl at the system level.
205	Use `install_train_unsloth.sh` instead — it layers the project on top without
206	re-resolving or clobbering those pre-installed versions:
207	
208	```bash
209	./scripts/install_train_unsloth.sh
210	```
211	
212	What it does:
213	
214	1. Creates `.venv-train` with `--system-site-packages` so the container's
215	   torch / Unsloth / vLLM are visible without redundant downloads.
216	2. Freezes the container packages as uv constraints (all versions locked).
217	3. Installs the project `src/` in editable mode (`--no-deps`) so that
218	   `from llm_workflow_agents import …` works without re-resolving the training
219	   stack.
220	4. Installs the project's base dependencies and dev tools under those
221	   constraints; packages already in the container are kept at their
222	   container-pinned versions.

file.content:
```bash
# Install uv (if not already installed)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Bootstrap the training environment (.venv-train)
./scripts/install_train.sh

# Bootstrap the inference / serving environment (.venv-infer)
./scripts/install_infer.sh
```

The installers call `uv venv` + `uv pip install -e ".[train,dev]"` (or
`".[infer,dev]"`) using the corresponding extras defined in
`pyproject.toml`. `scripts/install_train.sh` additionally pulls the
`vllm-0.19.1+cu130` wheel from the GitHub release (PyPI only ships the
cu129 build).

#### Unsloth pre-built container (`Dockerfile.unsloth`)

When using `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth` (based on `unsloth/unsloth`),
the container already ships torch, Unsloth, vLLM, and trl at the system level.
Use `install_train_unsloth.sh` instead — it layers the project on top without
re-resolving or clobbering those pre-installed versions:

```bash
./scripts/install_train_unsloth.sh
```

What it does:

1. Creates `.venv-train` with `--system-site-packages` so the container's
   torch / Unsloth / vLLM are visible without redundant downloads.
2. Freezes the container packages as uv constraints (all versions locked).
3. Installs the project `src/` in editable mode (`--no-deps`) so that
   `from llm_workflow_agents import …` works without re-resolving the training
   stack.
4. Installs the project's base dependencies and dev tools under those
   constraints; packages already in the container are kept at their
   container-pinned versions.

file.content:
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:1-3.12-bookworm

# Remove stale Yarn apt repo (ships with base image, has expired GPG key)
RUN rm -f /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list

# Update package lists and install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Upgrade pip and install uv
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && pip install uv

# Install Go
RUN wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
    && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
    && rm go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz
ENV GOPATH=/home/vscode/go
ENV PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin:$GOPATH/bin

# Install Go tools (protobuf, coverage merge, linting)
RUN go install google.golang.org/protobuf/cmd/protoc-gen-go@latest \
    && go install google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@latest \
    && go install github.com/wadey/gocovmerge@latest \
    && go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest \
    && chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode/go

# Install protoc (protobuf compiler)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends protobuf-compiler \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js using NodeSource repository (more reliable than NVM in Docker)
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | bash - \
    && apt-get install -y nodejs

# Verify installations
RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version

file.content:
# Unsloth official prebuilt image (Docker Hub: unsloth/unsloth).
#
# Per https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker, this image
# ships a ready-to-use Unsloth training stack (CUDA, torch, Unsloth,
# Jupyter on :8888, SSH on :2222, vLLM serve on :8000) at /workspace.
#
# We layer `uv` on top to match the `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer`
# workflow used by the other devcontainer images (Dockerfile,
# Dockerfile.cuda128).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   Run:     docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
#              -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
#              -v $(pwd):/workspace/ai-agent-llms \
#              --gpus all ai-agent-llms-unsloth

FROM unsloth/unsloth

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

USER root

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.text:
Yes, it makes sense. The pattern is established: `Dockerfile.unsloth` layers `uv` on top of the official `unsloth/unsloth` image. A `Dockerfile.tensorrt` would do the same with NVIDIA's official TRT-LLM base image, which ships CUDA 13, MPI, and torch pre-installed — solving the `libopenmpi-dev` dependency at build time rather than leaving it as a runtime pre-flight check.

The main tradeoff: NVIDIA's TRT-LLM container images are on NGC (`nvcr.io`), which requires an NVIDIA account/API key to pull, whereas `unsloth/unsloth` is public Docker Hub. If your team already has NGC access (likely for H100 work), this is a non-issue.

Want me to add it? The Dockerfile itself would be short — `FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorrt-llm/release:<tag>` + install `uv` + `git`/`curl`, mirroring `Dockerfile.unsloth` exactly. I'd also update `install_trtllm.sh` to drop the `dpkg -l libopenmpi-dev` pre-flight check (it's guaranteed in the container) and add a note to `devcontainer.json.example` showing how to switch to it.

1	# Unsloth training environment — CUDA 12.8 base.
2	#
3	# Why a separate image:
4	# The default .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses CUDA 13.0, which is what
5	# Phase 3 / Phase 4 (vLLM 0.20.0, turboquant_*) target. Unsloth's
6	# training stack (torch 2.10 + cu130 wheels) hits a driver mismatch
7	# on hosts running NVIDIA driver 570.x: every cublasGemmEx call
8	# returns CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE because driver 570 only fully
9	# supports CUDA 12.8 (CUDA 13 requires driver 575+ for native, or
10	# the cuda-compat-13-0 forward-compat package). Symptoms:
11	#   RuntimeError: CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE when
12	#   calling `cublasGemmEx(... CUDA_R_16BF ...)`
13	# fired on every linear layer in the model.
14	#
15	# CUDA 12.8 is natively supported by driver 570.x — no compat shim
16	# needed. Pair this image with cu128 torch wheels in .venv-train
17	# (Unsloth auto-installer recommends `unsloth[cu128-torch2100]`).
18	#
19	# Usage:
20	#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
21	#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.unsloth" when
22	#   you need to run Phase 2 SFT/GRPO training, then back to "Dockerfile"
23	#   for Phase 3/4 inference work.

26	
27	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
28	
29	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
30	    git \
31	    curl \
32	    ca-certificates \
33	    python3 \
34	    python3-pip \
35	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
36	
37	COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/
38	
39	ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

file.content:
# Unsloth training environment — CUDA 12.8 base.
#
# Why a separate image:
# The default .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses CUDA 13.0, which is what
# Phase 3 / Phase 4 (vLLM 0.20.0, turboquant_*) target. Unsloth's
# training stack (torch 2.10 + cu130 wheels) hits a driver mismatch
# on hosts running NVIDIA driver 570.x: every cublasGemmEx call
# returns CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE because driver 570 only fully
# supports CUDA 12.8 (CUDA 13 requires driver 575+ for native, or
# the cuda-compat-13-0 forward-compat package). Symptoms:
#   RuntimeError: CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE when
#   calling `cublasGemmEx(... CUDA_R_16BF ...)`
# fired on every linear layer in the model.
#
# CUDA 12.8 is natively supported by driver 570.x — no compat shim
# needed. Pair this image with cu128 torch wheels in .venv-train
# (Unsloth auto-installer recommends `unsloth[cu128-torch2100]`).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.unsloth" when
#   you need to run Phase 2 SFT/GRPO training, then back to "Dockerfile"
#   for Phase 3/4 inference work.

FROM nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Bootstrap the TensorRT-LLM serving virtualenv (.venv-trtllm).
3	#
4	# TensorRT-LLM exposes an OpenAI-compatible HTTP server via `trtllm-serve`
5	# on port 8000, used as a vLLM/SGLang alternative for Phase 1 and Phase 3
6	# concurrency benchmarks.
7	#
8	# PREREQUISITES (pip cannot satisfy these — install before running):
9	#   sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev
10	#
11	# Why a separate venv:
12	#   - TRT-LLM pins torch==2.10.0, conflicting with .venv-infer (≥2.11).
13	#   - Cannot coexist with .venv-train (Unsloth pins same torch but different
14	#     CUDA stack).
15	#
16	# Two serving modes (controlled via serving.source in the YAML):
17	#   source: "hf"          — trtllm-serve <hf_id>; JIT builds the engine on
18	#                           first launch (5-15 min for 30B-class models).
19	#                           KV-cache fp8 is NOT available in this mode.
20	#   source: "engine_dir"  — trtllm-serve <path>; uses a pre-built TRT engine.
21	#                           Run scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh (follow-up PR)

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap the TensorRT-LLM serving virtualenv (.venv-trtllm).
#
# TensorRT-LLM exposes an OpenAI-compatible HTTP server via `trtllm-serve`
# on port 8000, used as a vLLM/SGLang alternative for Phase 1 and Phase 3
# concurrency benchmarks.
#
# PREREQUISITES (pip cannot satisfy these — install before running):
#   sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev
#
# Why a separate venv:
#   - TRT-LLM pins torch==2.10.0, conflicting with .venv-infer (≥2.11).
#   - Cannot coexist with .venv-train (Unsloth pins same torch but different
#     CUDA stack).
#
# Two serving modes (controlled via serving.source in the YAML):
#   source: "hf"          — trtllm-serve <hf_id>; JIT builds the engine on
#                           first launch (5-15 min for 30B-class models).
#                           KV-cache fp8 is NOT available in this mode.
#   source: "engine_dir"  — trtllm-serve <path>; uses a pre-built TRT engine.
#                           Run scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh (follow-up PR)
#                           to build engines with fp8 KV cache.
#
# Usage:
#   sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev   # once, as root
#   ./scripts/install_trtllm.sh
#
# Re-running is safe (uv pip install is idempotent).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENV="$PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm"
CU130_INDEX="https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu130"

cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"

# ── Pre-flight checks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Checking prerequisites ..."

# libopenmpi-dev is required by TRT-LLM's mpirun-based multi-process model.
# Even at tp_size=1 it spawns child processes via MPI.
if ! dpkg -l libopenmpi-dev > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: libopenmpi-dev is not installed." >&2
    echo "       Run: sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev" >&2
    echo "       Then re-run this script." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA toolkit 13.1. Warn on older drivers.
if command -v nvidia-smi > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    CUDA_VER=$(nvidia-smi | grep -oP "CUDA Version: \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" || true)
    if [[ -n "$CUDA_VER" ]]; then
        CUDA_MAJOR=$(echo "$CUDA_VER" | cut -d. -f1)
        if [[ "$CUDA_MAJOR" -lt 13 ]]; then
            echo "WARN: nvidia-smi reports CUDA $CUDA_VER. TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA 13.1+" >&2
            echo "      Ensure driver ≥575 is installed for native CUDA 13 support." >&2
        fi
    fi
fi

# ── 1. Virtual environment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if [[ ! -d "$VENV" ]]; then
    echo "Creating $VENV ..."
    uv venv "$VENV" --python 3.12
else
    echo "Reusing existing $VENV ..."
fi

# ── 2. Torch 2.10.0 (cu130) — pin before TRT-LLM to prevent uv from
#       picking a newer torch that conflicts with TRT-LLM's wheel deps. ─────────
echo "Installing torch==2.10.0 (cu130) ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    --index-url "$CU130_INDEX" \
    --index-strategy unsafe-best-match \
    "torch==2.10.0" "torchvision==0.25.0"

# ── 3. TensorRT-LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Freeze torch pin as constraints so TRT-LLM's own deps can't bump torch.
CONSTRAINTS_FILE="$(mktemp /tmp/trtllm_constraints_XXXXXX.txt)"
trap 'rm -f "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE"' EXIT
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip freeze | grep -i "^torch==" > "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" || true

echo "Installing tensorrt_llm==1.2.1 ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    --constraint "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" \
    "tensorrt_llm==1.2.1"

# ── 4. Project package + dev tools ───────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Installing project package (editable) + dev tools ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install -e . --no-deps
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    "pytest>=8.0" \
    "pytest-cov>=5.0" \
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.23" \
    "ruff>=0.6.0" \
    "mypy>=1.11" \
    "dvc[gs]>=3.0" \
    "pyyaml>=6.0"

# ── Smoke test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo
echo "Done.  Activate with:  source .venv-trtllm/bin/activate"
echo
"$VENV/bin/python" -c "
import importlib.metadata as m
for pkg in ('tensorrt_llm', 'torch'):
    try:
        print(f'  {pkg:<18} {m.version(pkg)}')
    except Exception:
        print(f'  {pkg:<18} (not found)')
" 2>&1 | tail -10

echo
echo "Launch a model (JIT-from-HF) with:"
echo "  ./serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml"
echo
echo "NOTE: First launch compiles the TRT engine (5-15 min for 30B models)."
echo "      Use scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh + source: engine_dir for"
echo "      pre-built engines with fp8 KV cache support."

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. I'll create `Dockerfile.tensorrt` following the `Dockerfile.unsloth` pattern (layer `uv` on top of the official NVIDIA image), then update `install_trtllm.sh` to detect the container and use `--system-site-packages` (skipping the MPI pre-flight and pip-install of torch/trtllm that are already in the image) — same pattern as `install_train_unsloth.sh`.

# TensorRT-LLM serving environment.
#
# Based on the official NVIDIA TRT-LLM release container from NGC, which ships:
#   - CUDA 13.1 + cuDNN + cuBLAS
#   - libopenmpi-dev  (required even at tp_size=1 — MPI is TRT-LLM's process model)
#   - torch 2.10.0+cu130  (pinned by TRT-LLM)
#   - tensorrt_llm 1.2.1  (pre-installed at /usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages)
#   - trtllm-serve CLI on PATH
#
# NGC access required to pull the base image (free account):
#   docker login nvcr.io
#   Username: $oauthtoken
#   Password: <NGC API key from https://ngc.nvidia.com/setup/api-key>
#
# Why a separate container from .venv-infer / .venv-train:
#   TRT-LLM pins torch==2.10.0, conflicting with .venv-infer (≥2.11).
#   libopenmpi-dev is an apt-level dep that cannot be satisfied by pip.
#   This image resolves both without touching other environments.
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt \
#                         -t ai-agent-llms-trtllm .
#   Run:     docker run --gpus all --network host --ipc host \
#              --ulimit memlock=-1:-1 \
#              -v $(pwd):/workspace -v /ephemeral/cache:/root/.cache \
#              -w /workspace ai-agent-llms-trtllm
#   First-time setup inside container:
#              ./scripts/install_trtllm.sh
#
#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.tensorrt" when
#   you need TRT-LLM serving, then back to "Dockerfile" (or Dockerfile.cuda128)
#   for vLLM / training work.

FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorrt-llm/release:1.2.1

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Signals to install_trtllm.sh that torch + tensorrt_llm are pre-installed
# at the system level; the script will use --system-site-packages and skip
# re-downloading those packages.
ENV TRTLLM_CONTAINER=1

USER root

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.content: File created successfully at: /workspaces/ai-agent-llms/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

fi

# TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA toolkit 13.1. Warn on older drivers.
if command -v nvidia-smi > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    CUDA_VER=$(nvidia-smi | grep -oP "CUDA Version: \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" || true)
    if [[ -n "$CUDA_VER" ]]; then
        CUDA_MAJOR=$(echo "$CUDA_VER" | cut -d. -f1)
        if [[ "$CUDA_MAJOR" -lt 13 ]]; then
            echo "WARN: nvidia-smi reports CUDA $CUDA_VER. TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA 13.1+" >&2
            echo "      Ensure driver ≥575 is installed for native CUDA 13 support." >&2
        fi
    fi
fi

# ── 1. Virtual environment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if [[ ! -d "$VENV" ]]; then
    echo "Creating $VENV ..."
    uv venv "$VENV" --python 3.12
else
    echo "Reusing existing $VENV ..."
fi

# ── 2. Torch 2.10.0 (cu130) — pin before TRT-LLM to prevent uv from
#       picking a newer torch that conflicts with TRT-LLM's wheel deps. ─────────
echo "Installing torch==2.10.0 (cu130) ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    --index-url "$CU130_INDEX" \
    --index-strategy unsafe-best-match \
    "torch==2.10.0" "torchvision==0.25.0"

# ── 3. TensorRT-LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Freeze torch pin as constraints so TRT-LLM's own deps can't bump torch.
CONSTRAINTS_FILE="$(mktemp /tmp/trtllm_constraints_XXXXXX.txt)"
trap 'rm -f "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE"' EXIT
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip freeze | grep -i "^torch==" > "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" || true

echo "Installing tensorrt_llm==1.2.1 ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    --constraint "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" \
    "tensorrt_llm==1.2.1"

# ── 4. Project package + dev tools ───────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Installing project package (editable) + dev tools ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install -e . --no-deps
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    "pytest>=8.0" \
    "pytest-cov>=5.0" \
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.23" \
    "ruff>=0.6.0" \
    "mypy>=1.11" \
    "dvc[gs]>=3.0" \
    "pyyaml>=6.0"

# ── Smoke test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo
echo "Done.  Activate with:  source .venv-trtllm/bin/activate"
echo
"$VENV/bin/python" -c "
import importlib.metadata as m
for pkg in ('tensorrt_llm', 'torch'):
    try:
        print(f'  {pkg:<18} {m.version(pkg)}')
    except Exception:
        print(f'  {pkg:<18} (not found)')
" 2>&1 | tail -10

echo
echo "Launch a model (JIT-from-HF) with:"
echo "  ./serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml"
echo
echo "NOTE: First launch compiles the TRT engine (5-15 min for 30B models)."
echo "      Use scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh + source: engine_dir for"
echo "      pre-built engines with fp8 KV cache support."

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENV="$PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm"
CU130_INDEX="https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu130"
IN_CONTAINER="${TRTLLM_CONTAINER:-0}"

cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"

if [[ "$IN_CONTAINER" == "1" ]]; then
    # ── Container mode (Dockerfile.tensorrt) ─────────────────────────────────
    # torch + tensorrt_llm are pre-installed at the system level by the NGC
    # base image. Use --system-site-packages so the venv inherits them without
    # re-downloading. Skip all apt / CUDA pre-flight checks.
    echo "Detected Dockerfile.tensorrt container — using system site-packages."

    # ── 1. Virtual environment (inherit system packages) ─────────────────────
    if [[ ! -d "$VENV" ]]; then
        echo "Creating $VENV (--system-site-packages) ..."
        uv venv "$VENV" --python 3.12 --system-site-packages
    else
        echo "Reusing existing $VENV ..."
    fi

    # Freeze all container packages as uv constraints so installing project
    # deps cannot clobber torch / trtllm / cuda-level packages.
    CONSTRAINTS_FILE="$(mktemp /tmp/trtllm_constraints_XXXXXX.txt)"
    trap 'rm -f "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE"' EXIT
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip freeze > "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE"

    # ── 2. Project package (editable, no deps — deps already in container) ───
    echo "Installing project package (editable, --no-deps) ..."
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install -e . --no-deps

    # ── 3. Project base deps + dev tools (pinned to container versions) ──────
    echo "Installing project dependencies under container constraints ..."
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
        --constraint "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" \
        "peft>=0.14.0" \
        "datasets>=2.19.0" \
        "networkx>=3.2" \
        "jsonschema>=4.21.0" \
        "numpy>=1.26.0" \
        "scipy>=1.12.0" \
        "matplotlib>=3.8.0" \
        "seaborn>=0.13.0" \
        "pydantic>=2.9.0" \
        "pyyaml>=6.0" \
        "structlog>=24.0.0" \
        "wandb>=0.17.0" \
        "google-genai>=1.70.0" \
        "anthropic>=0.88.0" \
        "python-dotenv>=1.2.2" \
        "pytest>=8.0" \
        "pytest-cov>=5.0" \
        "pytest-asyncio>=0.23" \
        "ruff>=0.6.0" \
        "mypy>=1.11" \
        "dvc[gs]>=3.0"
else
    # ── Bare-metal mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    if ! dpkg -l libopenmpi-dev > /dev/null 2>&1; then

        echo "       Then re-run this script." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA toolkit 13.1. Warn on older drivers.
    if command -v nvidia-smi > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        CUDA_VER=$(nvidia-smi | grep -oP "CUDA Version: \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" || true)
        if [[ -n "$CUDA_VER" ]]; then
            CUDA_MAJOR=$(echo "$CUDA_VER" | cut -d. -f1)
            if [[ "$CUDA_MAJOR" -lt 13 ]]; then
                echo "WARN: nvidia-smi reports CUDA $CUDA_VER. TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA 13.1+" >&2
                echo "      Ensure driver ≥575 is installed for native CUDA 13 support." >&2
                echo "      Alternatively, use .devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt." >&2
            fi
        fi
    fi

    # ── 1. Virtual environment ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if [[ ! -d "$VENV" ]]; then
        echo "Creating $VENV ..."
        uv venv "$VENV" --python 3.12
    else
        echo "Reusing existing $VENV ..."
    fi

    # ── 2. Torch 2.10.0 (cu130) — pin before TRT-LLM to prevent uv from
    #       picking a newer torch that conflicts with TRT-LLM's wheel deps. ───
    echo "Installing torch==2.10.0 (cu130) ..."
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
        --index-url "$CU130_INDEX" \
        --index-strategy unsafe-best-match \
        "torch==2.10.0" "torchvision==0.25.0"

    # ── 3. TensorRT-LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Freeze torch pin as constraints so TRT-LLM's own deps can't bump torch.
    CONSTRAINTS_FILE="$(mktemp /tmp/trtllm_constraints_XXXXXX.txt)"
    trap 'rm -f "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE"' EXIT
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip freeze | grep -i "^torch==" > "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" || true

    echo "Installing tensorrt_llm==1.2.1 ..."
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
        --constraint "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" \
        "tensorrt_llm==1.2.1"

    # ── 4. Project package + dev tools ───────────────────────────────────────
    echo "Installing project package (editable) + dev tools ..."
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install -e . --no-deps
    VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
        "pytest>=8.0" \
        "pytest-cov>=5.0" \

        "ruff>=0.6.0" \
        "mypy>=1.11" \
        "dvc[gs]>=3.0" \
        "pyyaml>=6.0"
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap the TensorRT-LLM serving virtualenv (.venv-trtllm).
#
# TensorRT-LLM exposes an OpenAI-compatible HTTP server via `trtllm-serve`
# on port 8000, used as a vLLM/SGLang alternative for Phase 1 and Phase 3
# concurrency benchmarks.
#
# This script runs in two modes:
#
#   CONTAINER MODE (inside .devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt):
#     torch and tensorrt_llm are pre-installed system-wide by the NGC base
#     image. The script uses --system-site-packages and skips re-downloading
#     those packages. No apt pre-flight check is needed.
#     Detected automatically via the TRTLLM_CONTAINER=1 env var set in the
#     Dockerfile.
#
#   BARE-METAL MODE (any other environment):
#     PREREQUISITES (pip cannot satisfy these — install before running):
#       sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev
#     Installs torch==2.10.0+cu130 and tensorrt_llm==1.2.1 from scratch.
#
# Why a separate venv:
#   - TRT-LLM pins torch==2.10.0, conflicting with .venv-infer (≥2.11).
#   - Cannot coexist with .venv-train (Unsloth pins same torch but different
#     CUDA stack).
#
# Two serving modes (controlled via serving.source in the YAML):
#   source: "hf"          — trtllm-serve <hf_id>; JIT builds the engine on
#                           first launch (5-15 min for 30B-class models).
#                           KV-cache fp8 is NOT available in this mode.
#   source: "engine_dir"  — trtllm-serve <path>; uses a pre-built TRT engine.
#                           Run scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh (follow-up PR)
#                           to build engines with fp8 KV cache.
#
# Usage (bare metal):
#   sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev   # once, as root
#   ./scripts/install_trtllm.sh
#
# Usage (inside Dockerfile.tensorrt devcontainer):
#   ./scripts/install_trtllm.sh              # TRTLLM_CONTAINER=1 auto-set
#
# Re-running is safe (uv pip install is idempotent).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENV="$PROJECT_ROOT/.venv-trtllm"
CU130_INDEX="https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu130"

cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"

# ── Pre-flight checks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Checking prerequisites ..."

# libopenmpi-dev is required by TRT-LLM's mpirun-based multi-process model.
# Even at tp_size=1 it spawns child processes via MPI.
if ! dpkg -l libopenmpi-dev > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: libopenmpi-dev is not installed." >&2
    echo "       Run: sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev" >&2
    echo "       Then re-run this script." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA toolkit 13.1. Warn on older drivers.
if command -v nvidia-smi > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    CUDA_VER=$(nvidia-smi | grep -oP "CUDA Version: \K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" || true)
    if [[ -n "$CUDA_VER" ]]; then
        CUDA_MAJOR=$(echo "$CUDA_VER" | cut -d. -f1)
        if [[ "$CUDA_MAJOR" -lt 13 ]]; then
            echo "WARN: nvidia-smi reports CUDA $CUDA_VER. TRT-LLM 1.2.1 requires CUDA 13.1+" >&2
            echo "      Ensure driver ≥575 is installed for native CUDA 13 support." >&2
        fi
    fi
fi

# ── 1. Virtual environment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if [[ ! -d "$VENV" ]]; then
    echo "Creating $VENV ..."
    uv venv "$VENV" --python 3.12
else
    echo "Reusing existing $VENV ..."
fi

# ── 2. Torch 2.10.0 (cu130) — pin before TRT-LLM to prevent uv from
#       picking a newer torch that conflicts with TRT-LLM's wheel deps. ─────────
echo "Installing torch==2.10.0 (cu130) ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    --index-url "$CU130_INDEX" \
    --index-strategy unsafe-best-match \
    "torch==2.10.0" "torchvision==0.25.0"

# ── 3. TensorRT-LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Freeze torch pin as constraints so TRT-LLM's own deps can't bump torch.
CONSTRAINTS_FILE="$(mktemp /tmp/trtllm_constraints_XXXXXX.txt)"
trap 'rm -f "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE"' EXIT
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip freeze | grep -i "^torch==" > "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" || true

echo "Installing tensorrt_llm==1.2.1 ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    --constraint "$CONSTRAINTS_FILE" \
    "tensorrt_llm==1.2.1"

# ── 4. Project package + dev tools ───────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Installing project package (editable) + dev tools ..."
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install -e . --no-deps
VIRTUAL_ENV="$VENV" uv pip install \
    "pytest>=8.0" \
    "pytest-cov>=5.0" \
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.23" \
    "ruff>=0.6.0" \
    "mypy>=1.11" \
    "dvc[gs]>=3.0" \
    "pyyaml>=6.0"

# ── Smoke test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo
echo "Done.  Activate with:  source .venv-trtllm/bin/activate"
echo
"$VENV/bin/python" -c "
import importlib.metadata as m
for pkg in ('tensorrt_llm', 'torch'):
    try:
        print(f'  {pkg:<18} {m.version(pkg)}')
    except Exception:
        print(f'  {pkg:<18} (not found)')
" 2>&1 | tail -10

echo
echo "Launch a model (JIT-from-HF) with:"
echo "  ./serving/launch.sh configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b_trtllm.yaml"
echo
echo "NOTE: First launch compiles the TRT engine (5-15 min for 30B models)."
echo "      Use scripts/build_trtllm_engines.sh + source: engine_dir for"
echo "      pre-built engines with fp8 KV cache support."

input.old_string: **TensorRT-LLM apt prerequisite:** `sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev` must be installed before running `install_trtllm.sh`. The script checks for it and exits with a hint if it's missing.
input.new_string:
**TensorRT-LLM devcontainer:** `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt` is the recommended way to run TRT-LLM. It is based on the official NVIDIA TRT-LLM NGC image (`nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorrt-llm/release:1.2.1`), which pre-installs CUDA 13.1, `libopenmpi-dev`, and `tensorrt_llm` itself. Swap `"dockerfile": "Dockerfile.tensorrt"` in `devcontainer.json` to use it. An NGC account is required to pull the image — generate a free API key at https://ngc.nvidia.com/setup/api-key, then `docker login nvcr.io`.

**TensorRT-LLM bare-metal apt prerequisite:** If not using the devcontainer, `sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev` must be installed before running `install_trtllm.sh`. The script checks for it and exits with a hint if it's missing.

lines: -**TensorRT-LLM apt prerequisite:** `sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev` must be installed before running `install_trtllm.sh`. The script checks for it and exits with a hint if it's missing.

+**TensorRT-LLM devcontainer:** `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt` is the recommended way to run TRT-LLM. It is based on the official NVIDIA TRT-LLM NGC image (`nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorrt-llm/release:1.2.1`), which pre-installs CUDA 13.1, `libopenmpi-dev`, and `tensorrt_llm` itself. Swap `"dockerfile": "Dockerfile.tensorrt"` in `devcontainer.json` to use it. An NGC account is required to pull the image — generate a free API key at https://ngc.nvidia.com/setup/api-key, then `docker login nvcr.io`.

+**TensorRT-LLM bare-metal apt prerequisite:** If not using the devcontainer, `sudo apt-get install -y libopenmpi-dev` must be installed before running `install_trtllm.sh`. The script checks for it and exits with a hint if it's missing.

**KV-cache quantization vocabulary:** The vLLM-only quant strings (`turboquant_*`, `rotorquant_*`, `kivi_*`, `kvquant*`) are rejected by `launch_sglang.sh` and `launch_trtllm.sh` with a clear error. Phase 3 KV-quant benchmarks (`run_exp_d.sh`) are intentionally vLLM-only.
**SGLang tool-call parsers:** SGLang uses different parser names than vLLM. The per-backend YAMLs already carry the correct value — do not copy `tool_call_parser` between vLLM and SGLang configs.

input.description: Verify all dispatch tests still pass

content.text:
All 63 pass. Here's what was added:

**`.devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt`** — mirrors `Dockerfile.unsloth`: `FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorrt-llm/release:1.2.1` + installs `uv` + sets `TRTLLM_CONTAINER=1`. The NGC base image provides CUDA 13.1, `libopenmpi-dev`, and a pre-installed `tensorrt_llm`/`torch` stack, so none of that needs to be pip-installed.

**`scripts/install_trtllm.sh`** — now branches on `$TRTLLM_CONTAINER`:
- **Container path** (`=1`): uses `--system-site-packages` to inherit the container's torch/trtllm, freezes them as uv constraints, installs only the project + base deps. No MPI or CUDA checks.
- **Bare-metal path** (default): existing behavior — pre-flight `dpkg`/`nvidia-smi` checks, installs torch + trtllm from scratch. The MPI error hint now also mentions `Dockerfile.tensorrt` as an alternative.

To use the devcontainer, swap `devcontainer.json` to `"dockerfile": "Dockerfile.tensorrt"` and authenticate once: `docker login nvcr.io` with `$oauthtoken` / NGC API key.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(serving): add SGLang and TensorRT-LLM backends with devcontainer support

- serving/launch.sh: unified dispatcher reading serving.engine from YAML;
  execs launch_vllm.sh, launch_sglang.sh, or launch_trtllm.sh accordingly
- serving/launch_sglang.sh: SGLang 0.5.11 launcher (port 8000, triton backend,
  KV-quant vocab guard)
- serving/launch_trtllm.sh: TRT-LLM 1.2.1 launcher (JIT-from-HF + engine_dir
  modes, 30-min health-poll budget)
- serving/_yaml_helper.sh: shared parse_yaml() extracted from launch_vllm.sh
- scripts/install_sglang.sh: bootstraps .venv-sglang (torch≥2.11+cu130,
  sglang≥0.5.11)
- scripts/install_trtllm.sh: bootstraps .venv-trtllm; dual-mode — bare-metal
  (apt pre-flight + full pip install) and container (--system-site-packages,
  skips torch/trtllm reinstall)
- .devcontainer/Dockerfile.tensorrt: FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorrt-llm/release:1.2.1
  + uv; sets TRTLLM_CONTAINER=1 so install_trtllm.sh enters container mode
- 38 new per-backend YAML configs (14 exp_a + 5 exp_bc) × (sglang + trtllm);
  Nemotron configs carry skip_reason for Mamba-hybrid incompatibility
- scripts/run_exp_a.sh: dispatch through launch.sh, --backend flag, process-
  group kill, extended health poll for TRT-LLM, engine token in result filenames
- scripts/run_concurrency_benchmark.sh: same dispatch + engine-aware defaults
- scripts/run_exp_d.sh: comment clarifying intentional vLLM-only scope
- eval/agent_benchmark.py, eval/concurrency_benchmark.py: widen --engine
  choices; VRAM sampling enabled for all local backends
- pyproject.toml: [sglang] and [trtllm] optional-dep extras
- README.md: Serving Backends section with backend table, quick-start, caveats,
  parser mapping; Setup section updated to four-venv layout
- tests/test_serving_launch_dispatch.py: 63 tests covering executable bits,
  bash syntax, YAML dispatch, field validation, and vLLM regression guard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Create commit with all multi-backend serving changes
